articles.title: Iran And U.S. Agree To Halt Attacks And Renew Talks, U.S. Official Says
articles.content:
华盛顿/开罗6月28日电（路透社）——美国官员周日表示，伊朗和美国同意停止在海湾地区最近的敌对行动，并就霍尔木兹海峡争端恢复谈判，这为挽救一份承受多日针锋相对打击压力的临时和平协议带来了希望。

该官员称：“技术层面的谈判将就谅解备忘录的所有领域继续进行。双方目前将暂停行动，船只可以自由通行。”他指的是6月17日达成的14点谅解备忘录，根据该备忘录，海峡将重新开放通航。最先报道停火消息的美国Axios新闻网站援引一位美国高级官员的话称，谈判将于周二在卡塔尔恢复。

外交回归前，双方经历了数日的相互打击。自周四伊朗向霍尔木兹海峡一艘货船发射导弹以来，美伊双方互相指责对方违反了6月17日达成的临时停火协议。伊朗于周日早些时候向科威特和巴林的美军基地发射了导弹和无人机，此前不久特朗普总统曾威胁称，如果伊朗不遵守结束战争的协议，伊斯兰共和国将不复存在。

与此同时，以色列周日表示，再次袭击了黎巴嫩境内受伊朗支持的武装真主党激进分子，摧毁了该组织在黎巴嫩南部一个村庄使用的地下基础设施。此前的周六已发动了另一轮袭击，而就在周五刚刚与黎巴嫩达成了最新的停火协议。伊朗表示，如果更广泛的协议要得以维持，黎巴嫩的战斗必须结束。

美军早些时候表示，在霍尔木兹海峡一艘油轮遭袭数小时后，再次对伊朗发动了打击。霍尔木兹海峡是全球最重要的能源运输通道，德黑兰在冲突期间大部分时间基本上封锁了该海峡。

“如果到了某个时刻，我们无法再保持理性，将被迫以军事手段完成我们非常成功地开始的这项工作，”特朗普在Axios报道之前在社交媒体上表示。

“如果发生这种情况，伊朗伊斯兰共和国将不复存在！”他补充道。这份14点临时和平协议旨在停止战斗——美国和以色列于2月28日发动了这场战争——并在谈判继续讨论伊朗核计划等问题的同时重新开放海峡。

**和平协议后的暴力与指责**

一周前，由副总统万斯和伊朗议会议长卡利巴夫在瑞士主持的一轮调解谈判举行，华盛顿方面暂停了对德黑兰的制裁，但此后战斗重新爆发并加剧。

在特朗普发帖约一小时后，科威特军方表示其防空系统正在应对导弹和无人机袭击，巴林方面则表示当地响起了警报。

伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队在一份声明中表示，其海军和空军已发动导弹和无人机行动，目标是科威特和巴林的美军基地。

据伊朗国家电视台Press TV报道，革命卫队表示美方的袭击违反了停火协议，并将“导致所有外交进程完全停止”。革命卫队海军司令部表示，美国在该地区的基地“将在未来几天经历地狱”。

一位美国官员向路透社证实伊朗已瞄准美国设施，并表示中东地区没有报告美国人员伤亡或美国基地重大损坏，但局势仍在发展中。

数小时后，巴林再次响起警报。当局表示，伊朗的袭击破坏了穆哈拉格省的一栋住宅楼，没有人员伤亡的报告。巴林敦促联合国安理会召开紧急会议，让伊朗承担责任。

科威特军方表示拦截了两枚弹道导弹，没有造成损坏或人员伤亡。另外，卡塔尔表示其一名国民在周六一艘船只失踪期间因弹片受伤后死亡。另一人在此事件中受伤。内政部表示，这是由于“该地区的军事行动”，但没有说明具体地点或归咎于任何一方。

（路透社分社报道；丹尼尔·特罗塔、威廉·马勒德和亚历山大·史密斯撰写；凯特·梅伯里、菲利帕·弗莱彻和桑吉夫·米格莱尼编辑）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

WASHINGTON/CAIRO, June 28 (Reuters) - Iran and the United States agreed to halt recent hostilities in the Gulf and renew talks regarding their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, a U.S. official said on Sunday, raising hopes of saving an interim peace deal that was under pressure from days of tit-for-tat strikes.

“Technical talks are slated to continue on all areas of the MOU. Both sides will stand down for now, and vessels can move freely,” the official said, referring to the 14-point memorandum of understanding that was agreed on June 17 under which the strait would be reopened for traffic. Axios, which first reported the cessation of hostilities, citing a senior U.S. official, said talks would resume Tuesday in Qatar.

Advertisement

A return to diplomacy would follow several days of strikes and counterstrikes since an Iranian projectile hit a cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, with both the U.S. and Iran accusing the other of breaking an interim ceasefire that was agreed to on June 17. Iran launched missiles and drones at U.S. military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain early on Sunday, shortly after President Donald Trump threatened that the Islamic Republic would cease to exist if it did not honor the agreement to end the war.

Meanwhile, Israel said on Sunday it had once again struck Iran-backed armed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon, destroying underground infrastructure used by the group in a village in southern Lebanon. That came after another strike on Saturday, which closely followed its latest ceasefire deal with Lebanon on Friday. Iran says the fighting in Lebanon must end if the wider agreement is to stick.

The U.S. military said earlier it had struck Iran again, hours after a tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most important energy shipping route, which Tehran has largely closed for most of the conflict.

Advertisement

“There may come a point when we are no longer able to be reasonable, and will be forced to militarily complete the job that we very successfully started,” Trump said on social media, before the Axios report.

“If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!” he added. The 14-point interim peace accord was meant to halt the fighting, which the U.S. and Israel started on February 28, and reopen the strait while talks proceeded on issues such as Iran’s nuclear program.

VIOLENCE, ACCUSATIONS FOLLOW PEACE DEAL

One round of mediated talks, led by Vice President JD Vance and Iran’s parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, was held in Switzerland a week ago and Washington waived sanctions on Tehran, but fighting has since resumed and intensified.

Advertisement

About an hour after Trump’s post, Kuwait’s army said its air defenses were responding to missile and drone attacks, while Bahrain said sirens had sounded there.

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said in a statement its navy and air forces had launched missile and drone operations targeting U.S. military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain.

The Guards said U.S. strikes had violated the ceasefire and “will result in the complete halt of all diplomatic processes”, state-run Press TV said. The IRGC Navy command said American bases in the region “will experience hell in the coming days”.

Advertisement

A U.S. official, confirming Iran had targeted U.S. facilities, told Reuters there were no reported U.S. casualties or major damage to U.S. sites in the Middle East but the situation was still unfolding.

Hours later, alarms sounded for a second time in Bahrain, where authorities said an Iranian attack damaged a residential building in Muharraq province, with no casualties reported. Bahrain urged the U.N. Security Council to hold an urgent session to hold Iran accountable.

The Kuwaiti army said it had intercepted two ballistic missiles with no damage or casualties. Separately, Qatar said one of its nationals had died after sustaining injuries from shrapnel aboard a vessel that had gone missing on Saturday. A second person was injured in the incident, which was due to “military operations in the area”, the interior ministry said, without giving a location or apportioning blame.

Advertisement

(Reporting by Reuters bureaux; Writing by Daniel Trotta, William Mallard and Alexander Smith; Editing by Kate Mayberry, Philippa Fletcher and Sanjeev Miglani)

Related
united statesIran

articles.title: Death Toll From Venezuela Earthquakes Climbs To 1,450
articles.content:
加拉加斯6月28日（路透社）- 救援队周日争分夺秒搜寻本周两次强震的幸存者，废墟中偶尔出现的生命迹象为这场艰巨的搜寻任务带来了一丝慰藉。这场灾难导致数万人失踪。

截至周六，发生在周三的双震死亡人数已升至至少1450人，外国救援队纷纷涌入拉瓜伊拉州——这个已深陷政治和经济危机的国家中受灾最严重的地区。

在距加拉加斯以北约25英里的沿海地区，数十栋建筑坍塌成沙堆和瓦砾。

代理总统德尔西·罗德里格斯表示：“我们必须报告，死亡人数已达1450人，这些失去生命的男女同胞是因我国历史上所遭受的最残酷自然灾害而罹难。”

她说，约有3150人受伤，12721人无家可归，774栋建筑倒塌。

自前任总统在一月遭美军突袭被赶下台后，由罗德里格斯领导的政府此前感谢了将援助物资运往拉瓜伊拉的平民志愿者，但随后收紧了道路通行限制，称交通阻碍了紧急车辆的高效运转。

周日稍晚，国民议会议长、罗德里格斯的弟弟豪尔赫·罗德里格斯表示，救援队仍在积极寻找幸存者，争取最后的救援时间。

他在电视讲话中说：“每救出一条生命就是一个奇迹；每救出一条生命就是成千上万人的努力所得到的回应，我们将永远感激他们。”

他说：“我们现在处于关键而紧迫的时刻，需要继续营救生命，并建造收容营地，让那些失去家园或因各种原因无法返回住所的人们有地方可住。”

在2500多名外国救援人员抵达之前，家属和志愿者们已连续数日在废墟中搜寻幸存者和遇难者遗体。他们常常抱怨重型设备不足、官方力量有限。数百次余震不断加剧破坏，令居民惶恐不安。

截至本周末，政府表示截至周六晚间已救出至少33人，其中包括数名儿童，但仍有数万人下落不明。

尽管政府公布的失踪或被困人数为数百人，但周日当天反对派推广的一个网站显示，近5万人下落不明，较前一天的5.5万人略有下降。

**有限的救援时间**

美国地质调查局估计，震级分别为7.2级和7.5级的这两次地震可能造成超过1万人死亡，使其成为拉美近一个世纪以来最致命的地震之一。

从废墟中营救幸存者的时间正在流逝。

瑞士救援队领队塞巴斯蒂安·欧格斯特说：“大约存在三天、即72小时的窗口期，此后救出幸存者的概率会下降。”

他补充说，这支80人的救援队借助8只搜救犬在废墟中发现了多名生还者，但未能及时将他们救出以挽救生命。

周六晚间已超过地震发生后的72小时。

**被救出的儿童**

美国国务院高度赞扬美军救援人员周六救出一名婴儿的过程，在X平台上发布了一段视频，显示头戴头盔的救援人员将裹在毯子中、正在哭泣的婴儿从废墟中救出。

据路透社电视台报道，哥伦比亚救援队也救出了一名11岁男孩莫伊塞斯。他在废墟中约3米（10英尺）深处被困，救援队通过扫描仪确定了他的位置。

他躺在担架上被抬出，手臂骨折，眼睛被布遮住以保护其免受日光刺激。他的母亲和姐姐均已遇难。

墨西哥救援人员在卡拉瓦莱达镇一栋倒塌建筑中救出了另一名11岁男孩，罗德里格斯周六晚些时候在X平台上发布了一段视频，显示救援人员用担架将一个小小的身影从废墟中抬出。

教皇利奥周日对聚集在罗马参加圣母祈祷的信徒们表示，他“想向最近受地震影响的委内瑞拉兄弟姐妹们表达我的关切”，并向救援人员表示感谢。

一名美国高级官员周六表示，除特朗普政府已承诺的1.5亿美元外，预计将在未来一两天内宣布价值数亿美元的一揽子援助计划。

这场灾难可能对罗德里格斯的政治地位产生影响。尽管她在前任尼古拉斯·马杜罗手下担任副总统，但她一直将自己塑造成变革的推动者。

（加拉加斯分社的维维安·塞克拉和拉瓜伊拉分社的埃弗林·奥特罗报道；墨西哥城分社的迭戈·奥尔、波哥大分社的朱莉娅·西姆斯·科布、梵蒂冈分社的弗兰切斯卡·兰迪尼、班加罗尔分社的阿卡尼莎·库什补充报道；朱莉娅·西姆斯·科布和斯特凡尼·埃申巴赫执笔；比尔·伯克洛特、克里斯蒂安·普拉姆和克里斯·里斯编辑）
articles.summary:
CARACAS, June 28 (Reuters) - Rescue teams raced on Sunday to find more survivors of the two powerful earthquakes that struck Venezuela this week, with signs of life bringing occasional relief to a grim quest to whittle down a list of tens of thousands missing.

The death toll from Wednesday’s twin earthquakes rose to at least 1,450 as of Saturday as foreign rescue teams poured into La Guaira, the hardest-hit state of a country already mired in a deep political and economic crisis.

Advertisement

Dozens of buildings collapsed into piles of sand and rubble in the coastal state, about 25 miles north of Caracas.

“We must report that the number of fatalities has reached 1,450 people, women and men who lost their lives as a result of the most brutal natural catastrophe that our country has ever suffered in its history,” said interim President Delcy Rodriguez.

Some 3,150 people remained injured, 12,721 have been displaced, and 774 buildings have collapsed, she said.

Advertisement

The government — headed by Rodriguez since her predecessor was ousted by the U.S. in a January raid — had thanked civilian volunteers ferrying aid to La Guaira, but then tightened access to the road, saying traffic was preventing efficient movement of emergency vehicles.

Later on Sunday, Jorge Rodriguez, president of the National Assembly and brother of the interim president, said rescue teams remained active trying to find survivors before it is too late.

“Each life saved is a miracle; each life saved is the answer to the effort of thousands of people to whom we will be forever grateful,” he said in a televised speech.

Advertisement

“We are in critical hours, in crucial hours to continue rescuing lives and to build camps where those people who have lost their homes, or who cannot return, for whatever reason, to their residences can stay.”

Families and volunteers spent days pulling survivors and bodies from the rubble before the arrival of the more than 2,600 foreign rescue workers, often complaining of scant heavy equipment and a limited official presence, as hundreds of aftershocks deepened damage and kept residents on edge.

So far this weekend, the government said at least 33 people had been rescued by Saturday evening, including several children, while tens of thousands remained unaccounted for.

Advertisement

Although the government has given a figure of hundreds missing or trapped, just under 50,000 people were listed as unaccounted for on a website promoted by the country’s political opposition on Sunday, a slight decline from 55,000 people a day earlier.

LIMITED TIME FOR FINDING SURVIVORS

The U.S. Geological Survey estimated more than 10,000 deaths were possible from the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 quakes, which would place them among Latin America’s deadliest of the last century.

Advertisement

The clock is ticking for rescuing people still living amid the rubble.

“There exists a window of roughly three days, 72 hours, where the probability afterwards decreases that you can save people alive,” said Sebastian Eugster, the leader of the Swiss rescue team.

The 80-strong team had found multiple people alive in the rubble thanks to alerts from their eight search dogs, but had not been able to pull them out in time to save them, he added.

Saturday evening had already marked 72 hours since the quakes.

RESCUED CHILDREN

The U.S. State Department hailed the rescue of an infant by U.S. rescue crews on Saturday, posting a video on X showing helmet-clad rescuers removing the blanket-wrapped and wailing child from the rubble.

Advertisement

A Colombian rescue team also saved an 11-year-old boy, Moises, who had been trapped some 3 meters (10 feet) deep in rubble, after identifying his location with a scanner, Reuters TV reported.

He was removed on a stretcher with a broken arm, his eyes covered by cloth to protect them from the shock of daylight. His mother and sister were killed.

Mexican rescuers working at a collapsed building in the town of Caraballeda rescued another 11-year-old boy, Rodriguez posted on X late on Saturday, showing crews carrying a small figure on a stretcher out of the rubble.

Advertisement

Pope Leo on Sunday told worshippers gathered for the Angelus prayer in Rome that he wanted “to express my closeness to the Venezuelan sisters and brothers affected by the recent earthquakes” and expressed gratitude to rescue workers.

A senior U.S. official said on Saturday that a funding package worth hundreds of millions of dollars is expected to be announced within the next day or so, in addition to the $150 million the Trump administration had already committed.

The disaster could have political consequences for Rodriguez, who has portrayed herself as an agent of change even though she served as vice president under predecessor Nicolas Maduro.

Advertisement

(Reporting by Vivian Sequera in Caracas and Efrain Otero en La Guaira; Additional reporting by Diego Ore in Mexico City, Julia Symmes Cobb in Bogota, Francesca Landini in Vatican City, Akanksha Khushi in Bangalore; Writing by Julia Symmes Cobb and Stefanie Eschenbacher; Editing by Bill Berkrot, Christian Plumb and Chris Reese)

Related

Natural Disasters

Venezuela

venezuela earthquake 2026

Delcy Rodriguez

Stunning Photos Capture Horror Aftermath Of Twin Venezuela Earthquakes As Rescue Efforts Continue
Supreme Court's ‘Unjustifiable Decision’ Sparks Fears For U.S. Health Care System
Supreme Court Hands Trump A Major Win — Prompting Warnings Of ‘Violent, Needless Deaths’

articles.title: Lebanon's Deal With Israel Requires Hezbollah To Disarm. That Might Be Difficult.
articles.content:
贝鲁特（美联社）——真主党领导人周六批评了以色列和黎巴嫩前一天签署的框架协议，该协议旨在结束这个激进组织与以色列之间持续数月的冲突，此举引发了对协议有效性的担忧。

黎巴嫩和以色列周五在华盛顿签署了这项协议，真主党未参与其中。协议将以色列从黎巴嫩撤军与伊朗支持的激进组织解除武装挂钩，而真主党拒绝接受这一条件。

自最新一轮以色列-真主党战争爆发以来，黎巴嫩与以色列此前谈判达成的几项停火协议都未能在实地得到执行。

真主党领导人纳伊姆·卡塞姆在周六的声明中表示，该组织将继续战斗，直到以色列被迫离开黎巴嫩。协议宣布后，真主党支持者在贝鲁特街头举行抗议。

尽管协议已签署，黎巴嫩国家通讯社报道称，以色列无人机在南部城市纳巴提耶附近发动了袭击。

该通讯社还报道，以色列军方释放了周五在南部村庄艾因阿拉伯附近扣押的三名黎巴嫩工人和三名叙利亚工人。

协议要求以色列撤军但前提是真主党解除武装

美国国务院周六公布的协议细节显示，黎巴嫩和以色列的目标是最终结束自1948年以色列建国以来两国之间的战争状态。

协议规定，以色列将从黎巴嫩撤军，但前提是真主党必须解除武装。

协议要求以色列首先从两个被称为“试点区域”的小区域撤军，但未说明这两个初始区域的具体位置。黎巴嫩军队将逐步承担这些地区的全部安全责任。两国将商定未来以色列撤军的其他试点区域。

协议附有一份安全附件，其中包含黎巴嫩军队部署和以色列军队重新部署的细节。该安全附件未对外公布。

作为协议的一部分，以色列强调，真主党在整个黎巴嫩的解除武装，以及两国商定的其他安全措施，将消除以色列未来在黎巴嫩进行军事行动或驻扎的任何需要。

以色列国防部长以色列·卡茨表示：“协议确立的重要原则是，只要恐怖组织真主党不在整个黎巴嫩解除武装，以色列就不会在黎巴嫩南部重新部署，也不会撤军。”

卡茨补充说，以色列军方已接到指示，“准备在黎巴嫩安全区长期驻扎”。

以色列和黎巴嫩之间的谈判与本月早些时候美国和伊朗领导人签署的临时协议是分开的，后者旨在结束伊朗境内的战斗。

真主党领导人拒绝协议

卡塞姆周六表示，从真主党的角度来看，这份协议根本不存在。

他称该协议是“一种羞辱”，并指出将以色列撤军与真主党解除武装挂钩是“非常危险的提议”。

该协议促使真主党官员哈桑·法德拉拉发出警告称，这可能引发内战，因为真主党不会放弃武器，并将抵制黎巴嫩军队采取的任何措施。

黎巴嫩最高检察官艾哈迈德·拉米·哈吉法官周六指示该国安全机构负责人采取措施防止骚乱。

一些以色列人和黎巴嫩人对协议能否持久持怀疑态度

协议指出，黎巴嫩和以色列都认识到，通过黎巴嫩军队部署、其平民安全返回以及以色列北部社区的安全来恢复黎巴嫩南部安全，对长期稳定与和平至关重要。

在黎以边境的梅图拉镇参观的以色列公民罗尼特·贝尔森表示：“我个人认为这不会持久，因为黎巴嫩军队实际上根本无法与真主党抗衡。”

在黎巴嫩，人们对此看法不一。南部城市西顿居民拉比·萨穆尔说：“人们只是想好好休息一下。我支持黎巴嫩当局做出的决定。”

另一位西顿居民哈立德·加努姆则表示，该协议“使以色列的占领合法化”。

协议中有一段明显指向伊朗的文字——过去四十年间伊朗向真主党提供了数十亿美元资金。协议规定，黎巴嫩和美国承诺阻止资金流向任何与非国家武装团体有关的实体、组织或个人，并采取可用的法律措施来禁止任何此类实体、组织或个人的活动。

协议规定，黎巴嫩政府明确承诺阻止重建资金流向非国家武装团体及其关联实体。

_____

莫尔从以色列梅图拉报道。耶路撒冷的美联社记者易卜拉欣·哈兹布恩也参与了报道。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah’s leader on Saturday criticized a framework agreement that Israel and Lebanon signed a day earlier to end months of conflict between the militant group and Israel, raising concerns about its effectiveness.

Lebanon and Israel signed the deal in Washington on Friday without Hezbollah. The agreement links Israel’s withdrawal from Lebanon to the Iran-backed militant group’s disarmament, something Hezbollah rejects.

Advertisement

Several previous ceasefire agreements that Lebanon has negotiated with Israel since the outbreak of the latest Israel-Hezbollah war were never implemented on the ground.

In a statement Saturday, Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem said his group will keep fighting until Israel is forced to leave Lebanon. The group’s supporters protested in the streets of Beirut following the announcement of the agreement.

Despite the deal, the Lebanese state news agency reported an Israeli drone strike near the southern city of Nabatiyeh.

Advertisement

It also reported that the Israeli military released three Lebanese and three Syrian workers who were taken near the southern village of Ain Arab on Friday.

The Deal Calls On Israel To Withdraw But Only If Hezbollah Disarms

Details of the deal that the U.S. State Department released Saturday state that Lebanon and Israel aim to eventually end the state of war between them that began when Israel was created in 1948.

Advertisement

The deal says Israel will withdraw from Lebanon provided Hezbollah disarms.

It calls for Israel to initially withdraw from two small areas — called pilot zones. It did not say where those two initial zones will be. The Lebanese army will gradually assume full security responsibility over those areas. The two countries will agree to future pilot zones for Israel’s withdrawal in the future, the agreement says.

The deal has a security annex that includes the details of the deployment of the Lebanese army and redeployments of Israeli troops. The security annex was not made public.

As part of the deal, Israel stresses that the disarmament of Hezbollah throughout Lebanon and additional security measures to be agreed upon between the two countries will eliminate any future need for Israeli army’s military action or presence in Lebanon.

Advertisement

“The important principle established in the agreement is that there will be no redeployment by Israel in southern Lebanon, no withdrawal, as long as the terrorist organization Hezbollah is not disarmed throughout Lebanon,” said Israel’s Defense Minister Israel Katz.

Katz added that the Israeli military has been instructed “to prepare for an extended stay in the security zone” inside Lebanon.

The talks between Israel and Lebanon were separate from the interim deal that was signed earlier this month by the leaders of the U.S. and Iran to end the fighting in the Islamic Republic.

Advertisement

Hezbollah’s Leader Rejects The Deal

From Hezbollah’s point of view, the deal is nonexistent, Kassem said Saturday.

He called the agreement a “humiliation,” adding that linking Israel’s withdrawal to Hezbollah’s disarmament as a “very dangerous suggestion.”

The deal prompted one of the group’s officials, Hassan Fadlallah, to warn that it could result in civil war because Hezbollah won’t give up its weapons and will resist any measures taken by the Lebanese army.

Advertisement

Judge Ahmed Rami al-Hajj, Lebanon’s top public prosecutor, on Saturday told the heads of the country’s security agencies to take measures to prevent riots.

Some Israelis And Lebanese Are Skeptical That The Deal Will Last

The deal states that both Lebanon and Israel recognize that the restoration of security in southern Lebanon through the deployment of the Lebanese army, the safe return of its civilian population, and the security of Israel’s northern communities, are essential to long-term stability and peace.

“Personally, I don’t think it will be lasting because the Lebanese military cannot really stand a chance against Hezbollah,” said Israeli citizen Ronit Belson while visiting the town of Metula along the border with Lebanon.

Advertisement

In Lebanon, people were divided with Rabie Sammour, a resident of the southern city of Sidon saying: “People just want to rest for good. I support the Lebanese authorities in the decision” taken.

Another Sidon resident, Khaled Ghannoum, said the deal “legitimized Israel’s occupation.”

In an apparent reference to Iran, that has sent billions of dollars in cash to Hezbollah over the past four decades, the deal states that Lebanon and the United States commit to preventing funds from flowing to any entity, organization, or individual affiliated with non-state armed groups and to take available legal measures to proscribe the activity of any such entity, organization or individual.

The deal states that the Lebanese government explicitly commits to prevent reconstruction funds from flowing to non-state armed groups and connected entities.

Advertisement

_____

Mor reported from Metula, Israel. Associated Press journalist Ibrahim Hazboun contributed to this report from Jerusalem.
